rationale: The only ensures on Array::new is `ret.wf()` (i.e. `seq@.len() == N`), so we surface the length agreement of `r1.seq@` and `r2.seq@` in both guarded and unguarded forms (and via the inline `.view()` accessor) so that any trigger keyed on either form fires. Beyond this length fact, neither `seq` contents nor `ar` elements are pinned by `wf`, so the structural equality `r1 == r2` cannot be soundly derived from `r1.wf() && r2.wf()` alone.
